core.label: BASIC Fundamentals
core.description: The beginner-friendly language that started home computing.

topics.label: Setup (FreeBASIC/QB64)
topics.description: Install FreeBASIC or QB64 for modern BASIC. BASIC was designed for beginners in 1964.

topics.label: PRINT & INPUT
topics.description: PRINT for output, INPUT for user input. The first commands any BASIC programmer learns.

topics.label: Variables & Types
topics.description: DIM, INTEGER, SINGLE, DOUBLE, STRING. Variable names often end with $ for strings.

topics.label: Control Flow
topics.description: IF/THEN/ELSE/END IF, FOR/NEXT, WHILE/WEND, DO/LOOP, SELECT CASE.

topics.label: SUB & FUNCTION
topics.description: Define procedures with SUB and functions with FUNCTION. Structured programming.

topics.label: Arrays
topics.description: DIM array(size). One and multi-dimensional arrays with REDIM for resizing.

topics.label: File I/O
topics.description: OPEN, PRINT #, INPUT #, CLOSE. Read and write sequential and random files.

topics.label: Graphics
topics.description: SCREEN, LINE, CIRCLE, PSET, PAINT. BASIC was known for accessible graphics.
